---
title: "Diversity by dissemination area — Toronto"
description: "Population groups (incl. White & Indigenous) at Statistics Canada's finest standard geography, for the Toronto metro area (2021 Census)"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_groups_map, DIVERSITY_MAP_GROUPS
```

This map drops below the census tract to the **dissemination area (DA)** — Statistics Canada's *smallest* standard geography, about **400–700 people each** (several DAs make up one tract). Each little area is labelled by its StatCan ID — the "Statistics Canada Area #…" you may have seen (for example area **#35200654**). This page covers the **Toronto** metro; a [Vancouver version](neighbourhoods.qmd) is also available. There are ~57,000 DAs nationally, so they're built a metro at a time to keep the map light.

"Visible minority" is Statistics Canada's term, the map now adds explicit **White** and **Indigenous** categories (White is *derived* — "Not a visible minority" minus Indigenous identity; see the [method note](diversity.qmd#diversity-by-neighbourhood)). **Use the dropdown** to switch groups; zoom and pan to your street.

```{python}
#| echo: false
da_geo = "data/geo/da_toronto_2021.geojson"
da_path = "data/geo/statcan_da_toronto_ethnicity.csv"
if os.path.exists(da_geo) and os.path.exists(da_path):
    geo = json.load(open(da_geo))
    da = pd.read_csv(da_path, dtype={"dauid": str})
    groups = DIVERSITY_MAP_GROUPS
    fig = choropleth_groups_map(geo, da, "dauid", groups, name_col="name", breakdown_exclude=["all_vm"],
        pop_col="population", colorscale="Viridis", opacity=0.6, center={"lat": 43.72, "lon": -79.38}, zoom=8.5, height=720,
        source_note="Source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile 98-401-X2021006 — Toronto CMA.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Toronto DA diversity map not available — run build_da_profile(cmauid='535', pruid='35', geono='006_Ontario', slug='toronto').")
```

*Blank areas have no published data — either no residents (water, parks, industrial land) or a small but populated block whose detailed counts Statistics Canada suppresses for confidentiality. Download: [Toronto DA population-group shares (CSV)](../data/geo/statcan_da_toronto_ethnicity.csv) — 2021 Census (Statistics Canada).*

[← Back to Diversity](diversity.qmd)
